title: Asuka and Kaworu in Death and Rebirth
display_title: Asuka and Kaworu in Death and Rebirth
wikitext:
[]: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Sun Nov 13, 2011 2:12 am Hey guys, I know it's been a while, but I've been busy with "Evangelion Month" on my blog. I was re-watching "Death and Rebirth" for my review when something dawned on me: Why are Kaworu and Asuka practicing together in the auditorium? If memory serves me right, Kaworu was brought aboard as a replacement pilot when Asuka went off the mental reservation due to not being able to sync with Evangelion Unit-02. If this recital is prior to Asuka’s mental breakdown, then why the heck is Kaworu there? There is no reason for him to be there because Unit-02 has an active and seemingly healthy pilot. Shouldn’t he be at SEELE? Or, if this is after Asuka’s mental breakdown, why in the heck is she just waltzing into the auditorium like NOTHING happened, in school uniform, smiling and happy, ready to play her violin? This just seems weird to me. Could someone explain this? Did I miss something?

[]: Re: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Nov 13, 2011 2:31 am KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: Hey guys, I know it's been a while, but I've been busy with "Evangelion Month" on my blog. I was re-watching "Death and Rebirth" for my review when something dawned on me: Why are Kaworu and Asuka practicing together in the auditorium? If memory serves me right, Kaworu was brought aboard as a replacement pilot when Asuka went off the mental reservation due to not being able to sync with Evangelion Unit-02. If this recital is prior to Asuka’s mental breakdown, then why the heck is Kaworu there? There is no reason for him to be there because Unit-02 has an active and seemingly healthy pilot. Shouldn’t he be at SEELE? Or, if this is after Asuka’s mental breakdown, why in the heck is she just waltzing into the auditorium like NOTHING happened, in school uniform, smiling and happy, ready to play her violin? This just seems weird to me. Could someone explain this? Did I miss something?

[]: Re: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Sun Nov 13, 2011 2:33 am CJD wrote: The auditorium scene isn't "Real". It's just a device to bridge the parts of the story together.

[]: Re: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Nov 13, 2011 7:45 am CJD wrote: The auditorium scene isn't "Real". It's just a device to bridge the parts of the story together.

[]: Re: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Nov 13, 2011 12:17 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I keep thinking that as well, but that whole "18 Months Earlier" title card that's displayed as Shinji walks into that same auditorium for that same rehearsal seems to suggest that maybe it was real.

[]: Re: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Nov 13, 2011 12:29 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: It's never suggested that Kaworu didn't go to that school before his assignment to Unit 02. Though, it's never suggested that he did, either. (Other than the shot that just shows him strolling on in, and we're still not sure if there was anything literal behind that metaphor.) This is why it's so confusing to me from time to time, the movie doesn't even address the issue.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Nov 13, 2011 1:48 pm My pet theory is that it's part of an alternate parallel universe.

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Sun Nov 13, 2011 1:53 pm Yeah, I never took that scene as actually real. Just a bridge between the stories of the children. A recap mechanism. Link your blog? I'd like to hear the review

[]: Re: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 14, 2011 12:02 am Bagheera wrote: Don't read too much into that. The main narrative tells you everything you need to know; it isn't real.

[]: Re: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Nov 14, 2011 12:23 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Then why does the film explicitly say "18 Months Earlier" if the scene didn't take place 18 months earlier? It would just be an odd choice for a storyteller to blatantly say something happened if it never actually happened. The title card is really the only thing that keeps me second guessing every time I see the movie.

[]: Re: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 14, 2011 12:40 am Bagheera wrote: And yet everything else about the show -- absolutely everything -- tells you blatantly that there's nothing to it. You know Shinji wasn't there 18 months earlier. You know Asuka was in Germany at that point. You know Shinji, Rei, Asuka, and Kaworu had never seen one another by that point.

[]: Re: Asuka and Kaworu in "Death and Rebirth"             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Nov 14, 2011 12:53 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I guess knowing for sure how much time passes throughout the series would help me out on this one. Just how much time passes between Episode 8 and Episode 24 anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Nov 14, 2011 1:00 am ep09 concludes on September 11, 2015 - assuming that the calendar depicted in that episode is intended to be accurate (in other words, if someone was aware that September was the only month in 2015 where the 11th is a Friday). So between ep08 and ep24 is probably about four months. NGE has a fucked up timescale, yo. I've often wondered about the "eighteen months" thing in Death myself, but the only significance I could take from it was the "eighteen Angels" thing, which doesn't really make sense on any level.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Mon Nov 14, 2011 1:16 am I think that this event was simply an entertaining break from all the flashbacks and recaps Death contained. I do not think that it actually occurred, it was created simply to add feeling and interest to the film, with the added edition of music. Perhaps I have this view because I simply did not see the '18 months earlier' statement, I do not think the DVD had the correct settings in place while it played on my laptop to subtitle it. I also do not think Asuka and Kaworu were ever acquainted, as Asuka was in a comatose state in hospital at the time of Kaworu's arrival, and I also believe Shinji was the only musician out of the four children.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Nov 14, 2011 1:39 am ReiAyanami25 wrote: I also do not think Asuka and Kaworu were ever acquainted, as Asuka was in a comatose state in hospital at the time of Kaworu's arrival, and I also believe Shinji was the only musician out of the four children.

[]: Doesn't seem to be blocked. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Nov 14, 2011 1:46 am Bagheera wrote: but I doubt Kaworu would have the time to learn [/size]

[]: Re: Doesn't seem to be blocked. Bl             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Nov 14, 2011 1:54 am Alaska Slim wrote: Why not? I doubt SEELE sent him to a normal school, but he evidently had some appreciation for classical music, which would be even more natural if he had learned to play it on something.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Nov 14, 2011 2:42 am Bagheera wrote: I'm almost certain Asuka wouldn't have the patience for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Nov 14, 2011 2:49 am Azathoth wrote: Her response to Shinji's cello-playing in ep15 implies pretty strongly that she doesn't consider herself to have any musical inclination.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Mon Nov 14, 2011 2:51 am Bagheera wrote: She wasn't comatose.
